story: A baby boy arrived in Cleveland, Ohio, who would grow up to change brain surgery forever. Harvey Cushing was born on April 8, 1869. He became a famous doctor who studied how blood moves in our heads. Cushing's work helped make brain operations much safer for patients.
activity_question: What did Harvey Cushing study that helped make brain surgery safer?
activity_choices: How blood moves in our heads
How to make medicine taste better
activity_answer: How blood moves in our heads

title: Helen Joseph: Champion for Equality
story: Helen Joseph roared into the world on April 8, 1905. Born in England, she became a fierce fighter against apartheid in South Africa. Joseph bravely stood up for fairness and wrote powerful words in her book 'Side by Side'. Her courage inspired many to join the struggle for equality.

title: The Vitamin C Scientist
story: A brilliant chemist was born on April 8, 1907. Maurice Stacey entered the world in Moreton, England. He grew up to become a scientist who worked hard to create vitamin C in a laboratory. Stacey's research helped make this important nutrient more available to people everywhere.

title: The Birth of a Soul Music Legend
story: Leon Huff, a musical genius, entered the world on April 8, 1942. Born in Camden, New Jersey, he grew up to become a talented pianist and songwriter. Huff teamed up with Kenny Gamble to create the iconic 'Philadelphia Soul' sound. Together, they produced hits for famous artists like Billy Paul, The O'Jays, and Harold Melvin & The Blue Notes.
activity_question: What type of music did Leon Huff help create?
bonus_fact: Leon Huff's music influenced many artists and helped shape the sound of soul in the 1970s.

title: Dancing into Broadway History
story: Tap, tap, tap! On April 8, 1943, a future dance legend was born in Buffalo, New York. Michael Bennett would grow up to become a brilliant theater director and choreographer. His most famous work? The dazzling musical 'A Chorus Line', which wowed Broadway audiences with its energetic dance numbers and heartfelt stories of performers chasing their dreams.

title: The Accordion King of Tejano
story: Santiago Jiménez Jr. squeezed his way into the world on April 8, 1944, in Texas. This future accordion wizard would become a legend in Tejano and folk music. Known as 'El Mero Mero,' meaning 'the best of the best,' Jiménez mastered the squeezebox and delighted fans with albums like 'Viva Seguin.'
